paper_title: Analysis of Centennial-Scale Antarctic Climate Oscillations

methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing temperature-proxy and CO2 concentration data from ice cores for analysis.

steps.description: Open-access databases containing stable isotope records and computed planetary orbital parameters were analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch stable isotope records and orbital parameters from open-access databases

steps.description: Reduction of temperature-proxy and atmospheric CO2 concentration data to analytic format and color-coding of centennial-scale climate cycles.
steps.searchable_summary: process and color-code temperature-proxy and CO2 data

steps.description: Equalizing corresponding sampling resolutions by averaging (filtering) non-Vostok records.
steps.searchable_summary: average non-Vostok records to match Vostok sampling resolution

methods.description: Analysis of temperature-proxy oscillations to identify and describe Antarctic Centennial Oscillations (ACOs).

steps.description: Three definitions were used to describe temperature-proxy oscillations comprising ACOs.
ACO properties evaluated include period, amplitude, and warming and cooling duration and rate.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate ACO properties such as period and amplitude

methods.description: Application of spectral and statistical methods to analyze climate data and evaluate noise sources.

steps.description: Standard quantitative methodologies including Fourier analysis, auto-and cross-correlation, and t-tests were used.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Fourier analysis, auto-correlation, cross-correlation, and t-tests

steps.description: Evaluate major potential sources of noise in the reconstructed climate records.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate noise sources in climate records

steps.description: SAS JMP software used for spectral power analysis employs Fisher's Kappa Test Statistic as a white-noise test.
steps.searchable_summary: use Fisher's Kappa Test for white-noise analysis

steps.description: Compute confidence intervals of spectral density peaks using forward stepwise regression.
steps.searchable_summary: compute confidence intervals for spectral peaks
